chapter_title: Contracts for Enforcement of Certain Arrest Warrants
text:
In this chapter:

(1) “Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.

(2) “Registration” of a motor vehicle includes a renewal of the registration of that vehicle.

(3) “Traffic law” means a statute or ordinance, a violation of which is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine not to exceed $200, that regulates, on a street, road, or highway of this state:

(A) the conduct or condition of a person while operating a motor vehicle; or

(B) the condition of a motor vehicle being operated.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 702. Contracts for Enforcement of Certain Arrest Warrants
##### § 702.001 — Definitions

In this chapter:

    (1) “Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.

    (2) “Registration” of a motor vehicle includes a renewal of the registration of that vehicle.

    (3) “Traffic law” means a statute or ordinance, a violation of which is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine not to exceed $200, that regulates, on a street, road, or highway of this state:

        (A) the conduct or condition of a person while operating a motor vehicle; or

        (B) the condition of a motor vehicle being operated.

*Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 933 (H.B.3097), Sec. 2S.01, eff. September 1, 2009.*

subsections.text: “Department” means the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.
“Registration” of a motor vehicle includes a renewal of the registration of that vehicle.
“Traffic law” means a statute or ordinance, a violation of which is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine not to exceed $200, that regulates, on a street, road, or highway of this state:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: the conduct or condition of a person while operating a motor vehicle; or

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: the condition of a motor vehicle being operated.
